prediction_reason.历年位次与招生计划: 2021–2025年上海市综合类最低位次分别为8481→8160→9864→4662→4467，2023年因不限选科组拉低整体位次至9864，2024年起选科要求统一为物理、化学(2科必选)，位次大幅前移至4662，2025年继续小幅前移至4467，近两年累计前移约5397名，整体呈强势上升趋势；该校在上海市综合类招生计划2021年44人→2022年29人→2023年17人→2024年17人→2025年21人→2026年17人，2026年较2025年缩招4人（-19%），缩招幅度较大，理论上使竞争略有缓解。
prediction_reason.学校层次与声誉: 南京航空航天大学为211工程、双一流建设高校，软科中国大学排名2026年第37名，近年稳定在35-37名区间，属顶尖211层次；2025年高考周期内声誉偏正面，投档线稳步提升至211第3、全国第31名，入选国家卓越工程师学院，保研率提升至27%；2026年周期内因实验室爆炸火灾事故声誉偏负面，但整体关注度一般。抖音热度总排名第61位（评价一般），短视频曝光量中等；第五轮学科评估中A-及以上学科7个：力学A、机械工程A、管理科学与工程A、信息与通信工程A-、控制科学与工程A-、航空宇航科学与技术A-、软件工程A-，航空航天特色鲜明，工科实力突出。
prediction_reason.专业热度与地域: 电子信息/通信类2024年高热推荐，计算机类推荐（67.8），电气工程高热推荐（74.28），航空航天相关专业社会关注度持续升温；上海市2026年考生总数约66700人，较2025年49276人增加约35.4%，竞争显著加剧；省控线本科批稳定在400-405分，变化极小；南京航空航天大学位于江苏省南京市，与上海市地理邻近，长三角一体化背景下上海考生对江苏高校接受度较高；该校在上海市招生占比相对稳定。
prediction_reason.预测结论: 综合2024-2025年位次强势前移趋势、2026年招生计划缩招（-19%）、上海市考生总数大幅增加（+35.4%）、学校声誉正面为主、航空航天专业热度持续等因素，在，考虑考生总数激增带来的竞争加剧效应，预测2026年最低位次约4037名，区间3537-4537名。

major_tier_recommendation.tier_1:
一档（最难进）
- 航空航天类（含飞行器设计与工程等）
- 电子信息类（含电子信息科学与技术、信息工程）
- 低空技术与工程。
major_tier_recommendation.tier_2:
二档（中等）
- 自动化
- 无人驾驶航空器系统工程
- 机械工程
- 飞行器适航技术（拔尖创新班）。
major_tier_recommendation.tier_3:
三档（相对好进）
- 飞行器适航技术
- 人工智能
- 数字经济。
major_tier_recommendation.overall_advice: 预测位次约4037名，区间3537-4537名。位次在区间下限（3537名）以内可重点冲刺一档航空航天类、电子信息类等王牌专业；位次在区间中段（约4000名）建议以二档自动化、机械工程为主，搭配一档专业冲刺；位次在区间上限（4537名）附近建议以三档专业保底，同时关注二档中位次相对友好的专业。

历年位次与招生计划.title: Historical Rankings and Enrollment Plan
历年位次与招生计划.text: From 2021 to 2025, the minimum rankings for comprehensive subjects in Shanghai were 8481 → 8160 → 9864 → 4662 → 4467 respectively. In 2023, the unrestricted subject group lowered the overall ranking to 9864. Starting from 2024, the subject requirements were unified to Physics and Chemistry (both required), causing a significant forward shift to 4662. In 2025, it continued to move slightly forward to 4467, with a cumulative forward shift of about 5397 positions over the past two years, showing a strong upward trend. The enrollment plan for comprehensive subjects in Shanghai was 44 in 2021 → 29 in 2022 → 17 in 2023 → 17 in 2024 → 21 in 2025 → 17 in 2026. Compared to 2025, the 2026 plan reduces enrollment by 4 students (-19%), a relatively large reduction, theoretically easing competition slightly.

学校层次与声誉.title: University Level and Reputation
学校层次与声誉.text: Nanjing University of Aeronautics and Astronautics is a 211 Project and Double First-Class university. In the 2026 Soft Science China University Rankings, it ranks 37th, having been stable in the 35-37 range in recent years, placing it among the top-tier 211 universities. During the 2025 college entrance examination cycle, its reputation was generally positive, with admission scores steadily rising to 3rd among 211 universities and 31st nationally. It was selected for the National Excellent Engineer Academy, and the graduate school recommendation rate increased to 27%. In the 2026 cycle, due to a laboratory explosion and fire accident, its reputation turned somewhat negative, but overall attention was moderate. On Douyin, its overall popularity ranks 61st (average evaluation), with moderate short video exposure. In the fifth round of discipline evaluation, it had 7 disciplines rated A- or above: Mechanics A, Mechanical Engineering A, Management Science and Engineering A, Information and Communication Engineering A-, Control Science and Engineering A-, Aerospace Science and Technology A-, and Software Engineering A-. It has distinctive aerospace characteristics and strong engineering capabilities.

专业热度与地域.title: Major Popularity and Region
专业热度与地域.text: In 2024, electronics/communication majors were highly recommended, computer majors recommended (67.8), electrical engineering highly recommended (74.28), and aerospace-related majors saw continuously rising social attention. In 2026, the total number of examinees in Shanghai is about 66,700, an increase of about 35.4% compared to 49,276 in 2025, significantly intensifying competition. The provincial control line for the undergraduate batch remains stable at 400-405 points, with minimal change. Nanjing University of Aeronautics and Astronautics is located in Nanjing, Jiangsu Province, geographically close to Shanghai. Under the background of the Yangtze River Delta integration, Shanghai examinees have a relatively high acceptance of universities in Jiangsu. The university's enrollment proportion in Shanghai remains relatively stable.

预测结论.title: Prediction Conclusion
预测结论.text: Considering the strong forward trend in rankings from 2024-2025, the reduction in enrollment plan for 2026 (-19%), the significant increase in the total number of examinees in Shanghai (+35.4%), the generally positive reputation of the university, and the sustained popularity of aerospace majors, and taking into account the intensified competition due to the surge in examinees, the predicted minimum ranking for 2026 is approximately 4037, with a range of 3537-4537.

major_tier_recommendation_en.tier_1:
Tier 1 (Most Competitive)
- Aerospace (including Aircraft Design and Engineering, etc.)
- Electronics and Information (including Electronic Information Science and Technology, Information Engineering)
- Low-Altitude Technology and Engineering.
major_tier_recommendation_en.tier_2:
Tier 2 (Moderate)
- Automation
- Unmanned Aerial Vehicle Systems Engineering
- Mechanical Engineering
- Aircraft Airworthiness Technology (Top Innovation Class).
major_tier_recommendation_en.tier_3:
Tier 3 (Relatively Easier)
- Aircraft Airworthiness Technology
- Artificial Intelligence
- Digital Economy.
major_tier_recommendation_en.overall_advice: Predicted ranking approximately 4037, range 3537-4537. If your ranking is within the lower limit of the range (3537), you can focus on sprinting for Tier 1 majors such as Aerospace and Electronics and Information. If your ranking is in the middle of the range (around 4000), it is recommended to focus on Tier 2 majors like Automation and Mechanical Engineering, while also attempting Tier 1 majors. If your ranking is near the upper limit (4537), it is recommended to use Tier 3 majors as a safety net, while also considering Tier 2 majors with relatively friendly rankings.
